tasks.name: Book an afternoon family museum visit on a specific Saturday in May
tasks.description: Reserve general admission tickets for 2 adults and 1 child on Saturday, May 16, 2026, in a time slot starting at or after 3:00 pm, and reach the visit summary page showing that booking.
steps: Navigate to the museum website homepage.
Click the main navigation link labeled "Visit" in the header.
In the "Visit" section, click the "Tickets & Hours" option.
In the date picker on the tickets page, select Saturday, May 16, 2026.
In the ticket type section, select the "General Museum Entry" option.
From the list of available time slots for that date, click the earliest slot that starts at or after 3:00 pm (15:00).
In the ticket quantity fields, set "Adults" to 2 and set "Children (6–12)" to 1.
Click the button labeled "Add to Cart" or "Add to Visit" for that selected time slot.
Click the link or button labeled "View Cart" or "Visit Summary" to open the booking summary page.

tasks.name: Create a 4-stop self-guided tour of pre-1500 European cheese exhibits under 90 minutes total
tasks.description: Use exhibit filters to find European cheese history exhibits dated before 1500, choose 4 exhibits from 4 different European countries, each with an estimated visit time of 25 minutes or less, keeping the total estimated visit time at or below 90 minutes, and add them to a self-guided route.
steps: From the homepage, click the main navigation link labeled "Exhibitions" or "Galleries."
On the exhibitions page, click the button or link labeled "Explore Exhibits" or "Browse All Exhibits."
In the exhibit filters sidebar, set the region filter to "Europe" and set the era or time period filter to "Before 1500" or the equivalent pre-1500 option.
Use the sort dropdown (if available) to sort the results by "Estimated Visit Time" from low to high.
Scroll through the filtered exhibit list and open the first exhibit from a European country (e.g., Italy) whose estimated visit time is 25 minutes or less by clicking its title or image.
On that exhibit’s detail page, click the button labeled "Add to My Route" or "Add to Self-Guided Tour."

Use the back navigation or breadcrumb to return to the filtered exhibit list, and repeat the process to select 3 additional exhibits from 3 different European countries (e.g., France, Switzerland, and Greece), each with an estimated visit time of 25 minutes or less, adding each one to the same self-guided route.
After adding 4 exhibits, click the "My Route" or "Self-Guided Tour" link (often in the header or user panel) to open the route overview page.

On the route overview page, if a start time field is available, set the starting time to 10:00 am and, if offered, choose the option to keep the total walking time at or under 90 minutes (for example, by confirming or applying the suggested route optimization).

tasks.name: Choose the longest English guided tour under €25 on a specific date and start booking
tasks.description: Find guided tours available on August 10, 2026, in English, with a ticket price of €25 or less, select the tour with the longest duration among those options, and proceed to the booking details page for 1 adult ticket.
steps: Navigate to the museum homepage.
Click the main navigation link labeled "Tours & Workshops" or "Guided Tours."
On the tours page, locate the date selector and set the date to August 10, 2026.
In the language filter, select "English" so that only English-language tours are shown.
In the price filter, set the maximum price to €25 (for example, by entering 25 in a max price field or sliding a price range control to 25).
Use the sort dropdown to sort the filtered tour list by "Duration" from longest to shortest (e.g., "Duration – High to Low").
From the sorted list, click the first tour card (the longest available tour) to open its detailed page.
On the tour detail page, in the ticket selection area, set the number of adult tickets to 1 and leave other ticket categories (such as children or seniors) at 0.
Click the button labeled "Book Tour" or "Continue to Booking" to proceed to the booking details or summary page for that tour and ticket quantity.

tasks.name: Build a reading list of 3 short articles on cheese aging from 1800–1950
tasks.description: Locate educational articles about cheese aging and maturation techniques between 1800 and 1950, filter for articles with an estimated reading time of 10 minutes or less, and add the first 3 matching articles to a personal reading list.
steps: From the homepage, click the navigation link labeled "Learn" or "Resources."
On the learning resources page, click the section titled "Articles" or "Readings."
In the topic or category filter, select the option labeled "Aging & Maturation" or the closest match related to cheese aging.
In the time period filter, set the range to cover years from 1800 to 1950 (for example, by selecting a preset range or entering 1800 as the start year and 1950 as the end year).
In the reading time filter, set the maximum reading time to 10 minutes (e.g., by moving a slider to 10 or selecting a "10 minutes or less" option).
Apply the filters and, from the filtered list of articles, click the first article title to open its detail page.
On the article detail page, click the "Add to Reading List" or "Save for Later" button.
Use the back navigation to return to the filtered article list, and repeat the process to open and add the next two articles in the list (the second and third articles) to the reading list.
After adding 3 articles, click the "My Reading List" or "Saved Articles" link (often in the header or account area) to open the page showing your reading list.

tasks.name: Register a family (1 adult, 2 children) for a weekend cheese workshop for ages 8–12 in June under €40
tasks.description: Find a family-friendly cheese workshop suitable for children aged 8–12 that takes place on a weekend in June 2026, costs no more than €40 per participant, select a morning session, and proceed to the registration review page for 1 adult and 2 children.
steps: Navigate to the museum homepage.
Click the main navigation link labeled "Tours & Workshops" or "Programs."
On the programs page, switch to the "Workshops" tab if it is separate from tours.
In the calendar or date filter, set the month to June 2026 and restrict the day-of-week filter (if available) to weekends (Saturday and Sunday).
In the workshop type or audience filter, select the category labeled "Family & Kids" or specifically "Ages 8–12."
In the price filter, set the maximum price per participant to €40.
From the filtered list, click a workshop that matches these filters and offers a morning session (a start time before 12:00 pm) to open its detail page.
On the workshop detail page, in the session selector, choose a morning time slot on a weekend date in June 2026.
In the registration form, set the number of adult participants to 1 and the number of child participants to 2, then enter a group or contact name such as "Taylor Family" in the required name field.
Click the button labeled "Continue to Review" or "Proceed to Summary" to reach the registration review page showing the selected workshop, date, time, and participant counts.

tasks.name: Pin 5 timeline events (3 ancient, 2 modern) to a personal cheese history timeline
tasks.description: Use the interactive cheese history timeline to pin 3 events between 2000 BCE and 1 CE and 2 events between 1900 and 1950 to "My Timeline", and then open the personal timeline view.
steps: From the homepage, click the navigation link labeled "Timeline" or "Cheese Through Time."
On the interactive timeline, adjust the era range slider so that the start is set to 2500 BCE and the end is set to 1 CE (or 0 CE if that is the closest available value).
Within that visible range, scroll or pan along the timeline and click the first three distinct events whose dates fall between 2000 BCE and 1 CE to open each event’s detail popover.
For each of those three ancient events, click the "Pin to My Timeline" or "Add to My Timeline" button in the event popover before closing it.
Next, adjust the era range slider so that the start is set to 1900 CE and the end is set to 1950 CE.
Within the 1900–1950 range, scroll or pan along the timeline and click the first two events you encounter to open their detail popovers.
For each of those two modern events, click the "Pin to My Timeline" or "Add to My Timeline" button in the event popover.
After pinning a total of 5 events, click the "My Timeline" tab or link (usually near the top of the timeline page) to open the personalized timeline view showing the events you pinned.

tasks.name: Add 3 cheese history books to the cart with price and topic constraints and choose standard shipping
tasks.description: In the museum’s online shop, add 2 paperback books about European cheese history priced under $30 each and rated at least 4 stars, plus 1 paperback book about modern cheese science priced under $25 and rated at least 4 stars, then open the cart and select the standard domestic shipping option.
steps: Navigate to the museum homepage.
Click the navigation link labeled "Shop" or "Store."
On the shop page, click the "Books" category to view only book products.
In the format filter, select "Paperback" so that only paperback books are shown.
In the topic or subject filter, select the category labeled "European Cheese History" or the closest equivalent, and in the rating filter select items rated 4 stars or higher.
In the price filter, set the maximum price to $30, then from the filtered list add the first two qualifying books to the cart by clicking "Add to Cart" on each of the first two product cards.
Next, update the topic or subject filter to "Cheese Science" or "Modern Cheese Production" while keeping the format as "Paperback" and rating as 4 stars or higher.
Adjust the price filter so the maximum price is $25, then from the filtered results click "Add to Cart" on the first qualifying cheese science book.
After adding all three books, click the cart icon or the "View Cart" link in the header to open the shopping cart page.
On the cart page, in the shipping options section, select the "Standard Domestic" or "Standard (5–7 days)" shipping method from the available shipping choices.

tasks.name: Subscribe to the museum newsletter with specific cheese history interests and monthly frequency
tasks.description: Sign up for the museum’s email newsletter using the address 'visitor@example.com', selecting at least the interests 'Ancient Cheese History' and 'Museum Events & Workshops', choosing a monthly digest frequency, and completing any follow-up preference step if presented.
steps: Navigate to the museum website homepage.
Scroll to the footer and click the link labeled "Newsletter" or "Email Updates."
On the newsletter sign-up page, locate the interest checkboxes and select at least the options labeled "Ancient Cheese History" and "Museum Events & Workshops" (select both).
In the frequency or delivery dropdown, choose the "Monthly Digest" or "Once a Month" option.
In the email address field, type "visitor@example.com" as the contact email.
If a preferred language selector is present, choose "English" from the language dropdown.
Click the button labeled "Subscribe" or "Sign Up" to submit the newsletter subscription form.

If a follow-up preferences screen appears, leave the selected interests as they are and click the button labeled "Save Preferences" or "Finish" to complete the subscription process and reach the confirmation page.

entities.name: TicketType
entities.description: Types of museum admission tickets (e.g., General Museum Entry)

entities.name: TicketCategory
entities.description: Defines visitor ticket categories such as adults and children

entities.name: AdmissionTimeslot
entities.description: Bookable general admission time slots per day

entities.name: AdmissionBooking
entities.description: Selected museum entry booking that can be reviewed or added to cart

entities.name: Cart
entities.description: Unified cart / visit summary for tickets, programs, and shop products (single cart for the agent)

fields.name: items
fields.description: Array of CartItem IDs or embedded item objects

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Individual line items in the cart for tickets, bookings, or products

entities.name: Exhibit
entities.description: Cheese history exhibits / galleries in the museum

entities.name: SelfGuidedRoute
entities.description: Personalized self-guided tour route (My Route)

entities.name: RouteStop
entities.description: Stops (exhibits) included in a self-guided route

entities.name: Program
entities.description: Guided tours and workshops (shared metadata)

entities.name: ProgramSession
entities.description: Date/time-specific sessions for tours and workshops

entities.name: ProgramBooking
entities.description: Selected tour or workshop booking prior to confirmation

entities.name: Article
entities.description: Educational articles and readings in the Learn section

entities.name: ReadingList
entities.description: Personal reading list of saved articles (single list for the agent)

entities.name: ReadingListItem
entities.description: Join items connecting articles to the personal reading list

entities.name: TimelineEvent
entities.description: Events on the interactive cheese history timeline

fields.name: year
fields.description: Event year (negative for BCE). Use either year or start/end range.

entities.name: PinnedTimelineEvent
entities.description: Events pinned by the agent to the personal timeline view

entities.name: Product
entities.description: Shop products, including books on cheese history and science

entities.name: ShippingOption
entities.description: Available shipping methods for cart checkout

entities.name: NewsletterSubscription
entities.description: Newsletter subscription record for the agent with preferences

entities.name: NewsletterInterestOption
entities.description: Available content interest options for newsletter subscriptions

entities.name: SubscriptionInterest
entities.description: Interests selected within a newsletter subscription
